About Me

About Me

I'm a final-year B.Com (Hons.) Banking & Finance student at Jain (Deemed-to-be) University with a consistent CGPA of 7.5+ and 120+ hours of hands-on consulting experience across retail, F&B, and services.

I enjoy identifying real-world problems and transforming them into scalable solutions that create value for users and businesses. Winner of the University Entrepreneurship Award for building Attendie — an AI-powered attendance system.

I believe the best products are built at the intersection of user needs, business goals, and technology. Seeking a Business Analyst Internship at a top firm like McKinsey to contribute structured problem-solving and a growth-oriented mindset.

My Journey

My Journey

Started B.Com (Hons.) Banking & Finance
Enrolled at Jain (Deemed-to-be) University. Discovered the world of business, finance, and economics. Secured merit-level academic performance from day one.
Discovered Product Management
Fell in love with problem-solving and building digital products. Started self-studying PM frameworks, user research, and design thinking — not for grades, but out of genuine obsession.
Explored AI & Technology
Began learning AI tools and applying them to real-world business problems. Started building practical solutions and deepening knowledge of product strategy and technology.
Built Attendie & Competed
Designed and built Attendie — an AI-powered attendance system. Competed completely solo at Jain University's Entrepreneurship Event (500+ teams, 7,000+ audience). Won the Entrepreneurship Award. Recognised as Solopreneur.
Business Analysis & Research
Completed 120+ hours of hands-on consulting as a Business Analyst & Strategy Intern. Authored a 3,200-word research paper on AI vs Human Intelligence acknowledged at Jain University. Finalist at Entrepreneurship Cup. Now seeking a top Business Analyst internship.
Building at the intersection of Finance & Technology

04
Attendie Case Study

Attendie Case Study

05

The Problem

Traditional attendance systems are slow, manual and vulnerable to proxy attendance.

It leads to inaccurate records, wasted time and lack of transparency.

Key Pain Points
🔄
Proxy Attendance
Students can mark attendance for others.
⏱
Time Consuming
Manual process wastes valuable class time.
📉
No Real-time Data
Lack of insights and instant reporting.
Attendie — Solution

Attendie — Solution

Attendie solves this using a powerful combination of

QR Code + AI Face Recognition
for secure and accurate attendance.

1
Scan QR Code
Teacher generates a session-specific QR. Students scan with their mobile app.
2
AI Face Recognition
System verifies student identity using trained facial recognition model.
3
Verified & Recorded
Dual-verified attendance recorded in real-time dashboard instantly.
🔒
Secure
⚡
Instant
✅
Accurate
📊
Analytics
Student Login
Scan QR
Face Verified

My Story

From Bangaluru
to Building
Products

The journey of a Banking & Finance student who fell in love with solving real-world problems through technology.

Scroll to continue ↓
Chapter 01 · 2021

It started with numbers.

I was 16, sitting in a commerce class at MES Kishor Kendra, scoring 92% and wondering — why do businesses succeed while identical ones fail? That question never left me.

I dove into Maths, Stats, Business & Accountancy at Vidya Mandir PU College, scoring 90% in boards. But the real learning was happening in my head — pattern-matching how businesses worked, where they broke, what made them grow.

Foundation
MES Kishor Kendra → Vidya Mandir PU (MSBA, 90%) → Jain (Deemed-to-be) University BCom Hons Banking & Finance (CGPA 7.5+, 2024 – 2027)
Chapter 02 · 2022–2023

Then I discovered Product Management.

At Jain (Deemed-to-be) University, I found my tribe — people who built things. I discovered that the best products lived at the intersection of user empathy, business logic, and technology. That was exactly the triangle I'd been thinking about for years.

I started studying PM frameworks, user research, design thinking. Not for grades. Because I was genuinely obsessed.

🧠
PM Thinking
User-first mindset
📊
Data Analysis
Metrics & insights
🎨
Design Thinking
Empathy-driven
Chapter 03 · Real-World Experience

120+ hours in the field.

As a Business Analyst & Strategy Intern, I worked directly with small business owners across retail, F&B, and services. I didn't just observe — I diagnosed growth bottlenecks, ran competitor analyses, built strategy decks.

→
Conducted on-ground business audits for 12+ businesses — analysed Google presence, social media, competitors and built tailored roadmaps.
→
Recommended SEO optimisation, Instagram growth strategies, and local marketing tactics — helping clients improve walk-in conversions.
→
Received a formal Letter of Appreciation & Recommendation from clients for analytical depth and tangible business impact.
Chapter 04 · The Build

Then I built Attendie.

I noticed a real problem: attendance in college was a chaos — proxies, manual registers, zero data. I didn't complain. I built a solution.

Attendie — an AI-powered platform combining QR authentication and face recognition. Multi-factor. Proxy-proof. Real-time analytics for teachers.

A
Attendie
AI-Powered Smart Attendance Platform
🤖AI Face Recognition
📱QR Code Verification
📊Real-time Dashboard
📈Analytics & Reports
Chapter 05 · The Recognition

500+ teams.
7,000+ audience.
Only solo participant.

At Jain (Deemed-to-be) University's Entrepreneurship Event — I competed completely alone. Every other entrant had a team. I was the only individual to enter as a solo participant across both event tracks simultaneously. And I reached the finals.

🏆
Winner — Entrepreneurship Award
Selected from 500+ competing teams · Live audience of 7,000+
🎖️
Recognised Solopreneur
Only person to compete, present, and win individually — distinction no other participant received
🏁
Finalist — Entrepreneurship Cup
Top 10 from 60+ applicant teams · Only solo entrant in team-based competition
Chapter 06 · Research

I wrote about the future of work.

I authored a 3,200-word research paper analysing the socioeconomic impact of AI-driven automation on knowledge workers — drawing parallels with the Industrial Revolution's workforce displacement patterns.

The paper was recognised for practical insight and real-world relevance — acknowledged at Jain (Deemed-to-be) University's annual research showcase.

Research Paper
"AI Generation vs Computer Generation"
Socioeconomic impact of AI-driven automation on knowledge workers · Jain (Deemed-to-be) University Journal · 3,200 words
